steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content:
The shaded region is the set of points satisfying $rx + ty \geq -36$. The boundary line is $rx + ty = -36$. From the graph, the boundary line clearly crosses the y-axis at $(0, -12)$. Substitute this point into the equation:

$$r(0) + t(-12) = -36$$$$-12t = -36$$$$t = 3$$

steps.title: Find a second point on the boundary line
steps.content:
Tracing the line from left to right, identify another point on the boundary. The line passes through $(-12, -8)$ (visible as a marked point on the left side of the graph). Substitute into $rx + ty = -36$:

$$r(-12) + 3(-8) = -36$$$$-12r - 24 = -36$$$$-12r = -12$$$$r = 1$$

steps.title: Verify the line is correct, then sum
steps.content:
Check: The line $x + 3y = -36$ can be rewritten as $y = -\frac{1}{3}x - 12$, which has negative slope $-\frac{1}{3}$ — matching the downward-sloping boundary in the graph. Also verify the y-intercept: at $x = 0$, $y = -12$ ✓ and the second point: $-12 + 3(-8) = -36$ ✓.

Therefore, $r + t = 1 + 3 = 4$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
